original_datasets.name: data
original_datasets.columns: [DSM5_Total, MiniK_Total, Age, Sex, AAQ_01..AAQ_09, BPAS_01..BPAS_29, MiniK_01..MiniK_20, HKSS_01..HKSS_22, SH_01..SH_17, Aggresion_Total, HKSS_Total, SH_Total, Stepparent, Stepparent_N, ...]

summary_statistics.info: 138 rows x 231 columns; dtypes: float64=35, int64=110, str=86.

original_datasets.notes: Key variables used in the planned analysis are MiniK_Total (life history; higher=slower) and DSM5_Total (psychopathology severity; higher=more symptoms). No missing values in these two totals.

tasks.task_instruction: Conduct the analysis without any restrictions.

analysis_path.path_description: Compute Spearman correlation between MiniK_Total and DSM5_Total with one-sided alternative less (negative association expected if faster strategy relates to greater psychopathology).
preprocessing_steps: Remove observations with missing values on either variable.

key_choices.outcome_measure: DSM5_Total (sum of DSM-5 Self-Rated Level 1 symptom items).
key_choices.main_predictor_measure: MiniK_Total (sum score of Mini-K; higher=slower life strategy; lower=faster).
key_choices.missing_data_rule: Listwise deletion for the two variables.
key_choices.data_processing: No transformation; direct use of total scores.
key_choices.model_choice: Spearman rank correlation with one-sided alternative (less).

outcome.name: DSM5_Total
outcome.definition: Total psychopathology severity; higher scores reflect greater symptoms.

main_predictor.name: MiniK_Total
main_predictor.definition: Total Mini-K life history score; higher=slower strategy, lower=faster strategy.

analysis_code.notes: Python translation of the provided R analysis.Rmd. Script reads /app/data/data.csv and writes /app/data/results_ph39a.json.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Follow this planned path; do not change the focal claim, dataset, or analysis goal.

tasks.task_instruction: Report the Mini-K measure instead of HKSS for life history strategy.

analysis_path.path_description: Same as Task1: Spearman correlation between MiniK_Total and DSM5_Total using Mini-K as the life history measure.
preprocessing_steps: Remove observations with missing values on either variable.

key_choices.outcome_measure: DSM5_Total (sum of DSM-5 Self-Rated Level 1 symptom items).
key_choices.missing_data_rule: Listwise deletion for the two variables.
key_choices.data_processing: No transformation; direct use of total scores.
key_choices.model_choice: Spearman rank correlation with one-sided alternative (less).

outcome.name: DSM5_Total
outcome.definition: Total psychopathology severity; higher scores reflect greater symptoms.

main_predictor.name: MiniK_Total
main_predictor.definition: Total Mini-K life history score; higher=slower strategy, lower=faster strategy.

analysis_code.notes: Task2 uses the same Mini-K based correlation as Task1.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Use the Mini-K life history measure; do not substitute HKSS or change the model.

files.data/analysis__py.py: Python translation of the R analysis. Loads /app/data/data.csv, computes Spearman correlation between MiniK_Total and DSM5_Total (one-sided alternative='less'), prints JSON to stdout, and writes /app/data/results_ph39a.json.

codebase.notes: Original R code is provided as data/analysis.Rmd but per RUN POLICY execution is via the Python translation. Ensure the container mounts ./data to /app/data so the script can access data.csv and write outputs.

analysis.instructions: Execute Task1 and Task2 using the original dataset and the Python translation. Produce structured outputs (JSON) containing Spearman rho, two-sided p-value, one-sided p-value (alternative='less'), sample size, and variable names.
not_allowed_actions: replace the planned path with a different analysis path

debugging_rule.repair_scope: fix dependency, file path, or output extraction issues only; do not redesign the analysis.

analysis.comparison_metrics: Spearman rho, p-value (one-sided less), p-value (two-sided), N, and conclusion text about direction (negative association).
